I'm good.

on the 31st Pacific time, so they were locked in.

thought that you had.

having me.

Am I, am I getting that?
No, think that's, I think that's accurate.

We end up, you know, we need to talk to the attorneys to make deals happen, but paralegals are often some of our most involved users.

So we've generally tried to steer clear of that, but I don't know if that's evolved in the last couple of months.
No, I definitely want to steer clear of that for sure, of like productivity placement.
I think, um...
I think we're on it.
I think, yeah, we continue to build up like the thought leadership piece of it.

And then, yeah, we can talk with Pedro and see maybe for later if it's not in that, not in the productivity of like replacing paralegals or anything, but in terms of just topically not calling out our competitors, but how are differentiating aspects within our products of like what we do.
Yeah.
And we'll really need that for like, even up in Studio.
And I'm sure you've gotten the crash course on all those in the last couple of days, like that they're really well funded and they have really robust marketing arms out there already.

And so there's a lot that we can do to carve out space for ourselves by highlighting all these things, like the features that the team has pushed in the last like three months that aren't even on the website.
So I totally hear you there.
I think that's probably the right approach.
Yeah.
Awesome.
And I think like, I think Megan, the point you're making about So
So like everyone's talking about AI, right?

So actually we've done, I think we've done a really good job in like grabbing, like owning the conversation around like the specific features because I like, whereas when it comes to like broad legal AI, like AI is a huge space.
Every single company in every single like vertical is trying to like own AI, like automation, whatever the term might be.
So I really like the idea about like explaining it the way that you're phrasing.
I think that's cool.

And that's like a nice angle that's new and it allows us to, again, like let's try and carve out some more niches and grab the traffic of people who are more engaged and actually understand the product, probably get an idea themselves that like, hey, everyone's just saying AI, the eagle, like what does that actually mean?
I think that's quite powerful.
So, so, so,

I would say always, like, if you guys are having internal discussions or thoughts about how the product is, like, anything you can share with us that helps us learn more about differentiators is just, like, gold dust for us.
It makes everything better.
So, okay, I think, like, in the meantime, we have a clear roadmap to continue with, and we can build on that and change as we go along.
But retrieval, definitely, I want to keep enriching.
then to your point about, like, pagination and deduplicate, I guess that would fall, like, more into medical record review and, let's say, like, the chronology piece.
The chronology cluster is underdeveloped because we've been focusing on retrieval.
So there's definitely, like, roadmap for us there.
And there's definitely runway in the demand letters piece as well.
Like, we've only published one piece on that.
Like, we didn't prioritize.
So...

There's plenty of places I want to go and I have ideas about, and then as things develop on your side, whether that's new campaigns or new features that you're pushing and are finally on the website, we can quickly, we can always add that into the cadence.
So, yes, okay.
So we are also, oh yeah, another piece, we are looking into more case analysis candidates because they have been so powerful for traffic overall.
That's kind of an ongoing process, Megan, that we do.

There's obviously a lot out there, but things like Starbucks, et cetera.

And if you guys know, if anything comes across your plate being in the space, it's like, oh, there's this really high profile case at the moment, like, tell us because we can go and try and grab a share of that.
Yeah.
So rad.
And then just want to do a quick dive.
I'll do a proper January performance recap, Megan, towards the end of January when things have normalized a bit and we can look at how January performed.
But just checking in since we lost it, we had crazy weeks just before Christmas.
This was driven by the case analysis.
As expected, Christmas and New Year tends to be a little bit flatter, but like, again, engagement is super high, continuing to be super high.
And this, like, upward trajectory.
So we can see this is only halfway through the week.
This is measuring to the 11th of January.
So we're only halfway and we're almost a parity with last week.
So this is going to be up here.
We're back on, like, a nice upward curve.
And yeah, I would just reiterate again, like, what's really cool is just this engagement rate is fantastic.

And then looking at GSC, this would be across your whole site, but this is always nice to see a clear upward, up and right path across the site, a lot of which is being supported by some of the top pages, obviously your homepage, but then we have a fair few blogs in here that are top performing pages.
I appreciate the site, it's pretty small at moment, but what we know is like organic, the blog work that we're doing is driving this improvement rate.
So when we first started, I believe that was in, I want to say September now.
Yeah, I think it, well, my first day was September 1st, so I think it must have been like that week.
Yeah, yeah, so, so we're, we're going kind of like 35 clicks, um, 35 clicks a day, and then we're like, we're up around the mark now, so this is.
Pretty cool.
Impressions is going even quicker.
So I think, yeah, net is like, we've found it.
We've uncovered quite a solid strategy.
And now, like, let's apply that.

And let's keep drilling down on the case analysis piece because they are all like, that's just finding us a lot of traffic, building us authority as Pedro wanted.
So, yeah, that's everything I've got from you.
put a small wish list together, some of which we've described on the, like, features.

then, yeah, as you said, that would be like gold dust to like help make everything better and then contest Pedro's appetite for new capsules moving forward as well.

then that way we can get you on a much better summer evening schedule than you're on right now.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Handover to Megan and align on Q1/Q2 content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=715.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Strategy Confirmed:** Continue the successful "Retrieval-first" strategy, using high-traffic utility content (e.g., state-by-state clusters) to build authority and attract users for upsells.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=743.0)
  - [**New Focus:** Develop content on Tavrn's key differentiators—workflow, ease of use, and features like deduplication—to counter competitor "AI" claims without framing it as a paralegal productivity tool.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=844.0)
  - [**Performance:** Organic traffic grew from \~35 clicks/day to \~100 clicks/day since September, driven by content clusters and high-performing case analyses.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=1321.0)
  - [**Logistics:** Megan D'Arcy now leads the relationship; syncs move to bi-weekly starting Jan 22.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=1531.0)

## Topics

### Content Strategy & Performance

  - [**"Retrieval-first" Model:** Retrieval content is the primary acquisition channel, successfully driving hundreds of new customers. Other products (e.g., Chronology, Demand Letters) serve as the upsell pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=743.0)
  - [**Content Clusters:** The core strategy is building state-by-state clusters from high-traffic pillar pages.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=255.0)
      - [**Statute of Limitations:** This cluster finishes this week (5 articles).](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=193.0)
      - [**Proven Success:** Previous clusters (Malpractice Caps, Punitive Damages) showed high traffic and engagement, validating the model.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=289.0)
      - [**Future Candidates:** Paralegal requirements by state is a strong candidate for a new cluster.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [**Case Analyses:** These articles are a powerful traffic driver. GrowthX will continue identifying high-volume cases (e.g., Starbucks) to analyze.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=1222.0)

### New Content Focus: Differentiation

  - [**Goal:** Counter competitor "AI" claims by highlighting Tavrn's unique value.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=844.0)
  - [**Key Differentiators:**](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=805.0)
      - [Workflow orchestration & ease of use.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=851.0)
      - [Specific features like record deduplication and re-pagination.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=805.0)
  - [**Strategic Framing:**](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=851.0)
      - [Focus on product features, not broad "legal AI" topics.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=851.0)
      - [Avoid framing as a paralegal productivity tool to prevent job-displacement concerns.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=977.0)
      - [GrowthX needs detailed input on these differentiators to create robust content.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=890.0)

### Analytics & Logistics

  - [**Analytics:**](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
      - [**GA4:** Matt will investigate if the homepage "jump link" and "request demo" forms are tracked as the same "form start" event.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=484.0)
      - [**Looker:** The conversion event view tracks the full user funnel from session start to demo request.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [**Publishing Workflow:**](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=552.0)
      - [**Standard:** Content goes directly to publishing.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=552.0)
      - [**Review:** Tavrn reviews articles on sensitive topics or new feature descriptions.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=552.0)
  - [**Meeting Cadence:**](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=1531.0)
      - [**Ownership:** Megan D'Arcy is now the primary contact.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=1531.0)
      - [**Frequency:** Syncs will move to bi-weekly to allow more time for updates.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=1531.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=193.0)
      - [Finalize and publish the 5 remaining Statute of Limitations articles.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=543.0)
      - [Investigate GA4 tracking for the homepage "jump link" and "request demo" forms.](https://fathom.video/share/SAGsn6gzpghKkmm1HYEayj2CsEw3BWnn?tab=summary&timestamp=484.0)
